heading: Can I specify my own VPC for my MongoDB Atlas project?
body:
No. An Atlas project, and its clusters, are associated with a
region-specific VPC (Virtual Private Cloud).

Atlas creates a VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) when you deploy the first `M10+` dedicated
paid cluster to a given provider and region. For multi-region clusters,
Atlas creates one VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) per region if there is not already a VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)
for that region.

*(AWS deployments only)* Atlas also creates a VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) when you create
a VPC peering connection to an AWS (Amazon Web Services)
VPC (Virtual Private Cloud). Atlas creates the VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) in the same region as the peered
VPC (Virtual Private Cloud).

To use a different VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) (that is, on the customer's own cloud
infrastructure accounts), you would need to use MongoDB Cloud Manager
or Ops Manager.
